subject: grammar
title: Exercise — Would / Used To
instruction: A teacher and student are talking about local customs. Fill in the gaps with the verbs in brackets in the correct form. Use *would* or *used to* where possible.
passage:
Teacher: What sort of things did you use to do (you/do) as a child?
Yoko: Oh, when I was a child growing up in Japan there were many customs that we would/used to follow (follow). For example, I remember we moved (move) house when I was seven and we visited (visit) our new neighbours with gifts. At that time the tradition was that people would/used to give (give) gifts of Japanese noodles, but it is different now and people tend to give things like soap or towels or nothing at all.
Teacher: Did you use to have (have) one tradition that you particularly remember?
Yoko: Yes, one tradition that I really used to like (really/like) was in the spring when the cherry blossoms were out. As a family we used to/would go (go) into the countryside and we used to/would spend (spend) the day eating, drinking and singing. One year my father took (take) a lovely photo of me and my sisters and I still keep that picture on my wall today.
Teacher: And did you use to have to (you/have to) do anything you didn't like?
Yoko: Yes. I remember how we used to have to (have to) clean the house thoroughly. This ceremony is called Osoji and my sisters and I didn't use to look forward to (not/look forward to) it very much!

questions.text: What sort of things ___ (you/do) as a child?
For example, I remember we ___ (move) house when I was seven
...and we ___ (visit) our new neighbours with gifts.
At that time the tradition was that people ___ (give) gifts of Japanese noodles,
___ (have) one tradition that you particularly remember?
Yes, one tradition that I ___ (really/like) was in the spring when the cherry blossoms were out.
As a family we ___ (go) into the countryside
...and we ___ (spend) the day eating, drinking and singing.
One year my father ___ (take) a lovely photo of me and my sisters
And ___ (you/have to) do anything you didn't like?
questions.answer: did you use to have to

questions.text: Yes. I remember how we ___ (have to) clean the house thoroughly.
This ceremony is called Osoji and my sisters and I ___ (not/look forward to) it very much!
questions.answer: didn't use to look forward to
